title: uxselect
text: uxselect The uxselect widget is a listbox control that contains a list of possible values. You can select one or more values in a uxselect widget. Default Logical Widgets The default logical widget is ux-Select . Triggers (web)trigger onchange: This trigger is fired when the value is changed. Extends fluent-select Web Component This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-select. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-select web component. Keyboard support Vertical scrolling keys to navigate listbox Pressing the Enter key opens listbox Pressing the Enter key from listbox selects item and closes listbox Layout processing Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features. Widget properties The uxselect widget supports the following properties: Table 1. Widget Properties Property Description html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only. html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a select. html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled. class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class. display-format Defines how values are displayed by the widget. The available options are: rep : Displays the representation only. This is a default value. val : Displays the value only. valrep : Displays both value and representation. show-placeholder Defines whether to display a placeholder or not. placeholder-text Defines a placeholder text in case the value is null and property show-placeholder is true . The default text value is Select item . label-position Defines the position of the label with respect to its widget. The available options are: above (default) below before after Note: If the label-position is not defined for the widget, then it is aligned by default above the widget. popup-position Defines the position for the popup to open in a specified direction. The available options are: Above Below . This is a default value. Note: The popup's behavior depends on the viewport. For example - If a user defines popup-position=above , and if there is no space above for a select popup to open, it will open below, regardless of the defined position. This behavior is handled by Fluent. exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid . Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget. Helper and validation error visualization A uxselect widget deals with the following error visualizations: Red color border alert Alert icon (after suffix icon) Tool-tip when hovering the "Alert icon" showing the error message When the uxselect has focus, no red border is shown, only error icon is shown When the uxselect does not have a focus, both red border and error icon is shown A no-representation-error error is shown if the field value does not have a corresponding representation. This includes a null value if the property is not set. UI blocking When the field is in transaction, the uxselect widget blocks user input by becoming read-only. Widget styling Search: Column CSS Selector Selected node(s) Filter results... 27 rows found CSS Selector Selected node(s) Filter 27 rows found Filter 27 rows found CSS Selector Selected node(s) .u-select Widget node .u-select.open Widget node when the listbox is opened or expanded .u-select .u-error-icon Error icon node .u-select.disabled or . u-select[disabled] Widget node when the widget is disabled .u-select.u-invalid Widget node when the value is invalid .u-select.u-format-invalid Widget node when there is a format invalid .u-select::part(selected-value) Selected value node .u-select .u-valrep-representation Valrep representation node .u-select .u-valrep-value Valrep value node .u-select .u-placeholder Placeholder node .u-select::part(control) Control node .u-select::part(listbox) Listbox node .u-select::part(start) Start node .u-select::part(end) End node .u-select::part(selected-value) Selected value node .u-select::part(indicator) Indicator node .u-select fluent-option Option nodes .u-select fluent-option.selected Selected option nodes .u-select fluent-option::part(start) Start node of the option node .u-select fluent-option::part(end) End node of the option node .u-select fluent-option::part(content) Content node of the option node .u-select:active Widget node when the widget is active .u-select:focus Widget node when the widget has focus .u-select:focus-visible Widget node when the widget has focus-visible .u-select:hover Widget node when the widget is hovered .u-select[u-label-position= <position-value> ] Widget node with u-label-position=<position-value>. The available values for position-value are: above below before after .u-stretchable Defines how the widget stretches, allowing it to resize dynamically based on the layout rules. Widget sources The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/select . Related Links ux-Select This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-Select properties. UX Widgets Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
